_doc: PSA Set authoring template. Copy this file to your week folder as psa-set.json and fill in the fields below.
_id_doc: Unique string identifier for this set. Used as the localStorage key and in the dropdown. Convention: 'week-N' where N matches the folder name.
title: Topic Title Here
_title_doc: Short display name shown in the set dropdown.

labels._labels_doc: Array of label objects. Each label gets a color and shape assigned automatically by its position in this array (index 0, 1, 2, ...). No color or shape keys are needed — the app cycles through a fixed accessible palette.
labels._id_doc: Kebab-case string, unique within this set. Used as a stable key for tagging state.
labels.name: Display Name
labels._name_doc: Short label name shown on buttons and badges.
labels.desc: One sentence: what structural concept does this label identify?

_labels_count_doc: Tip: 6–10 labels works best. The palette supports up to 12 before cycling. Fewer labels = less cognitive load per prompt.

prompts.title: Short problem name (e.g. 'Salary Comparison')
prompts._title_doc: Displayed in bold in the prompt list and as a heading above the tokenized statement. Keep it short — 2-4 words.
prompts.statement: Scenario-based description of the problem. Write in third person, present tense. Describe the situation and what the program needs to do — do NOT prescribe code structure, variable names, or specific syntax. Students should infer the structure by tagging.
prompts._statement_doc: This replaces the old string prompt. Avoid imperatives like 'Create a class' or 'Add a method'. Frame it as a real-world scenario instead.
prompts.code: Clean, idiomatic Java solution. Newlines as \n, no actual line breaks in the JSON string value. Use proper idioms for the week's topic level.
prompts._code_doc: Revealed only after the student adds at least one tag. Should demonstrate the canonical solution — not the only solution, but a good one.

_prompts_doc: Array of prompt objects. 6–8 prompts per set is the target. Legacy string format is still supported for backward compatibility — the app normalises it automatically.
_legacy_prompts_doc: String format (legacy): 'Plain-English problem statement.' — still rendered correctly but shows no title and has no code reveal.
